_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
| Obligation | Deadline | Status | Next Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| All new planning permit applications for new buildings in City of Melbourne municipality subject to ESD policy review | Upon gazettal by Minister for Planning (date TBC — adopted 29 Jul 2025, awaiting gazettal) | Pending gazettal | Monitor Victoria Government Gazette weekly; flag all Melbourne permit applications |
| New buildings ≤5,000 sqm: must achieve minimum 50% BESS score (preferred 70%) | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Engage ESD consultant; prepare BESS assessment as part of Sustainable Design Assessment (SDA) |
| New buildings ≤5,000 sqm: must achieve minimum 60% in BESS Energy category (preferred 70%) | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Include BESS Energy sub-score in SDA |
| Residential new buildings: must achieve average 7.5-star NatHERS rating across dwellings; minimum 6.5-star per dwelling | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Commission NatHERS rating from accredited assessor at design stage |
| New buildings >5,000 sqm: must achieve minimum 5-star Green Star Buildings (preferred 6-star) | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Register project with Green Building Council of Australia; prepare Sustainability Management Plan (SMP) |
| All new developments and additions >1,000 sqm: must achieve minimum Green Factor score of 0.55 | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Submit Green Factor scorecard as part of landscape documentation |
| New buildings >5,000 sqm: must provide building/landscape elements covering at least 75% of site area to reduce urban heat island effect | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Incorporate green roofs, solar panels, non-reflective roofing, shading; document in SMP |
| Water: must achieve minimum 50% score in BESS Water category; mandatory connection to recycled water (if available) or install rainwater tank supplying ≥10% of building water demand | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Engage water retailer to confirm recycled water availability; integrate tank design |
| Waste: must provide waste and resource recovery facilities meeting City of Melbourne Guidelines for Waste Management Plans; must comply with precinct waste management plan if one applies | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Review applicable precinct WMP; prepare compliant waste management plan |
| Sustainable Transport (Capital City Zone and Docklands Zone): rates for car share, bicycle, motorcycle parking and EV provision apply | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Confirm zone classification; design parking to meet C376 transport schedule |
| Non-residential buildings >5,000 sqm: must enter NABERS Energy Commitment Agreement (typology-specific targets) | Applies to all new permits post-gazettal | Pending gazettal | Identify applicable NABERS energy target for building type; execute Commitment Agreement at permit stage |
| Controls do NOT apply to change of use without physical works, or to renovations adding <1,000 sqm GFA | Continuous | Not applicable | Confirm GFA threshold for any proposed works before assuming exemption |

*Source: How does this affect me? | Amendment C376: Sustainable Building Design, Participate Melbourne / City of Melbourne, 29 July 2025. https://participate.melbourne.vic.gov.au/amendment-c376/how-does-this-affect-me; Tract Consultants, Recent Changes to ESD Requirements — Amendment C376, March 2025. https://tract.com.au/recent-changes-to-esd-requirements-in-city-of-melbourne-amendment-c376/*
